subject: refactor(ui): tier-2 reductions — drop LoadingSpinner + OfflineIndicator wrappers, dedup close-icon, strip overhead memoization
body:
Second-pass audit findings applied after tier 1. Every change either
removes an abstraction that was carrying less weight than its file, or
strips `useMemo`/`useCallback` that couldn't prevent a re-render because
nothing downstream was memoized.

- LoadingSpinner.tsx deleted. The wrapper was a `flex items-center
  justify-center` container around `<span class="loading loading-spinner">`
  — exactly what the 3 call sites in App.tsx already wrapped it in.
  Inlining moves `role="status"` + `aria-label="Loading"` onto the outer
  container, drops one DOM node, and removes a one-consumer file.
- OfflineIndicator.tsx deleted. Single caller (AppLayout). The
  `navigator.onLine` subscription + conditional `alert-warning` banner
  are now inline in AppLayout, next to the other page-top system banners
  (auth-bypass warning, update-available, offline-ready, check-result).
- src/constants/icons.ts (new). `ICON_X` path constant dedups the 3
  copies of `d="M6 18L18 6M6 6l12 12"` across AppLayout / PwaInstallPrompt
  / NotificationBell. Mirrors AppLayout's local `ICON_MOON/SUN/...`
  pattern; multi-consumer icons live in constants, single-consumer icons
  stay next to their consumer.
- AppLayout menuItems: `useMemo` dropped. Zero `React.memo` in the
  codebase means the memoization couldn't protect any downstream
  component from re-rendering — it only added dep-array bookkeeping.
- ToneSelector handlers: `useCallback` dropped on `handleCustomSubmit`
  + `handleKeyDown`. Both pass to native `<input>` / `<button>`, which
  never memoize props, so the callbacks add no value. `useCallback`
  import removed from the file.

Kept with explicit justification (from audit): ConfirmDialog (semantic
abstraction worth the file), InstallInstructionsModal (137 lines of
domain copy — merging bloats consumer), useNotifications /
usePdfVerifications / useSignatureLog (intentional data-access layer
architecture).

Verified: `tsc -b` clean, `npm run lint` clean, `npm test` → 22 passing
(3 dist-level tests auto-skipped).

Tags: refactor, cleanup, daisyui, code-quality
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: custom-code-reduction
Semver: patch
